data.title: WD-40 shares hit 52-week low after Q1 earnings miss
data.description:
WD-40 (WDFC) shares slid on Friday, hitting a 52-week low, as the company posted first-quarter earnings that missed expectations, with management noting that sales came in below long-term growth targets and that a slower start to fiscal 2026 had been anticipated.

"The softness was driven by timing factors within our marketing distributor network, not by a decline in end-user demand. All indicators point to a strong rebound later in the fiscal year," CFO Sara Hyzer said on the conference call as the company reaffirmed its guidance. 

Regarding the household divestiture, the company said it continues to advance the potential sale of its Americas home care and cleaning product brands, with its investment bank engaged in discussions with multiple prospective buyers.

While no transaction is assured, management said it remains optimistic and will provide updates as appropriate.

WD-40 (WDFC) said "unanticipated inflationary headwinds and other unforeseen events" may affect its view of fiscal year 2026, and in the event, it is unsuccessful in the divestiture of the Americas homecare and cleaning brands, the guidance would be positively impacted by approximately $12.5 million in net sales, $3.6 million in operating income, and $0.20 in diluted EPS on a full-year basis.

Jefferies maintained its Hold rating and $229.00 price target on the company, while DA Davidson has reiterated its Buy rating and $300.00 price target.

Shares in the American manufacturer of household and multi-use products were last down -7.78% at $187.66.

data.title: U.S. stocks mostly higher after key labor data comes largely in line
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were largely higher on Friday as the key jobs report came in line with expectations. The indexes are on track for weekly gains after a week of losses.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.2% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was flat.

On focus today, the December nonfarm payrolls growth came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

“Friday's weaker-than-expected jobs report showed that hiring continued to slow towards the end of 2025, capping a tough year for the labor market. Although the market is still questioning the soundness of the jobs data given the recent disruptions from the government shutdown, throughout 2025, the labor market faced many challenges, including uncertainties from tariffs to artificial intelligence,” said Dennis Follmer, CIO at Montis Financial.

“Higher for longer on interest rates has been the name of the game for an economy continuing to surprise to the upside. As long as that economic strength continues, the market’s expectations for rate cuts are likely to prove overly optimistic,” Follmer said.

Also, the January consumer sentiment index also came in slightly higher than expected and higher than December, according to the University of Michigan Survey of Consumers.

Over in the bond market, Treasury yields advanced across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.18%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.52%.

In addition, investors await the Supreme Court's decision on the legality of President Donald Trump's tariffs. According to the apex court's website, it might come on Friday.

Also, Trump said he has called off plans for a second wave of military attacks on Venezuela, citing the release of political prisioners and calling it "a very important and smart gesture."

data.title: Heron rises as preliminary revenue figures exceed estimates
data.description:
Heron Therapeutics (HRTX) added ~14% on Friday after the company announced its preliminary and unaudited net revenue figures for Q4 and full-year 2025, exceeding Street forecasts.

The Cary, North Carolina-based biotech said it expects its Q4 and full-year net revenue to reach nearly $40.5M and $154.9M compared to $39.8M and $154.15M projected by analysts, respectively.

Notable topline contributors include Zynrelef, Heron’s (HRTX) extended-release local anesthetic, which added $12.5M with a ~35% sequential rise during Q4, its largest quarter-over-quarter revenue growth.

First approved in 2021 for postsurgical analgesia after bunionectomy, open inguinal herniorrhaphy, and total knee arthroplasty, the Zynrelef label was expanded by the FDA in 2024 for soft tissue and orthopedic surgical procedures.

“In addition to our revenue growth, we are extremely pleased with the continued progress made across all aspects of our business throughout the past year,” CEO Craig Collard added.

data.title: These consumer stocks are falling after the Supreme Court does not issue a tariff ruling
data.description:
The consumer sector is seeing some selling action on Friday after the Supreme Court did not rule on if the White House exceeded its authority by using a 1977 emergency law to levy sweeping import duties on trade partners. The duties were imposed via the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, which is a 1977 law that allows the U.S. President to regulate economic transactions during a declared national emergency.

Decliners during the morning session included Mattel (MAT) -4.7%, Hasbro (HAS) -3.0%, Nike (NKE) -1.1%, Lululemon (LULU) -2.9%, Under Armour (UAA) -5.2%, PVH (PVH) -5.0%, Fossil (FOSL) -7.1%, and Deckers Outdoor (DECK) -3.1%.  Many of those stocks are giving back notable gains from earlier in the week.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: INTC, QCOM, OKLO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged up Friday as investors digested an in-line monthly jobs report and eyed a potential White House ruling on aggressive import tariffs.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Vistra (VST) and Oklo (OKLO) +15% - Shares advanced after Meta (META) announced three major nuclear-power agreements to secure up to 6.6 GW of carbon-free energy for its data centers by 2035. The company signed 20-year PPAs with Vistra covering more than 2,600 MW from three existing Ohio and Pennsylvania reactors, supporting what Vistra said will be the largest nuclear uprates ever backed by a corporate buyer. Meta also struck separate deals to support future small modular reactor projects from Oklo and TerraPower, including Oklo’s 1.2 GW planned campus in Ohio and TerraPower’s planned 690 MW deployment as early as 2032, along with rights to future reactors.
  • Intel (INTC) +8% - Shares soared after President Trump said he had a “great meeting” with CEO Lip-Bu Tan, praising Intel’s launch of what he called the first sub-2nm CPU designed and built in the U.S. Trump also highlighted the government’s equity stake in Intel, claiming it has generated “tens of billions of dollars” in gains within four months, and framed the partnership as part of a broader push to restore leading-edge chip manufacturing in America. The comments followed Intel’s disclosure earlier this week that it shipped its first 18A-based products, Panther Lake chips under the Intel Core Ultra Series 3 line.
  • Tilray Brands (TLRY) +2% - Shares rose after reporting FQ2 2026 results that topped revenue expectations, marking its highest quarterly revenue on record. Cannabis revenue rose 3%, while international cannabis sales surged 36% Y/Y, helping drive the beat. The company reaffirmed its full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance and said the U.S. push toward marijuana rescheduling, recently directed by President Trump, could open the door for expanded opportunities in medical cannabis.

Biggest stock losers

  • iHeartMedia (IHRT) -10% - Shares plunged to their lowest level since Nov. 4 after Goldman Sachs downgraded the stock to sell from neutral. Analyst Stephen Laszczyk cited the stock’s sharp +123% run-up in 2025 and mounting concerns that shifting audio consumption and weaker ad trends are limiting the company’s ability to sustain growth across its multi-platform audio business. With $5.1B in debt maturing through 2031 and free cash flow running below expectations this year, Laszczyk cut his price target to $3.50.
  • AXT (AXTI) -6% - Shares dropped after the semiconductor wafer maker cut its Q4 2025 revenue outlook to $22.5M–$23.5M, well below the prior $27M–$30M range and the $28.77M consensus. The company cited fewer-than-expected export control permits for indium phosphide from China’s Ministry of Commerce. CEO Morris Young said they are working to secure additional permits in early 2026 and remain on track to more than double indium phosphide capacity in the second half of the year. He added that the recent capital raise will support further capacity expansion amid strong long-term data center demand.
  • Olin (OLN) -4% - Shares dipped after the company cut its Q4 adjusted EBITDA outlook to ~$67M, sharply below the prior $110M–$130M range and missing the Street’s expectations. CEO Ken Lane said results were hurt late in the quarter by a steep decline in chlorine demand and operational disruptions at the company’s Freeport, Texas, facility due to a planned turnaround and a third-party raw-material supply interruption. He noted that the site is now back to normal operations and emphasized the company’s continued focus on safety, cost reductions, and a disciplined value-first commercial strategy.
  • Qualcomm (QCOM) -3% - Shares fell after Mizuho downgraded the stock to Neutral from Outperform and cut its price target to $175 from $200, citing handset headwinds and expected share losses in Apple (AAPL) devices this year and next. While cautious on Qualcomm, Mizuho maintained a broadly positive view on the semiconductor sector for the first half of 2026, pointing to sustained AI spending, strong wafer fab equipment demand, and an improving memory cycle. However, the firm warned of potential volatility in the second half of 2026 tied to major product launches, the U.S. midterm elections, and a slower Fed rate-cut cycle.

data.title: BMO downgrades Adobe to Market Perform on increasing competition, no positive catalysts
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) was downgraded to Market Perform from Outperform by BMO Capital Markets as the financial firm believes shares will remain range-bound due to increasing competition and no perceptible positive catalysts on the horizon.

BMO also reduced its price target to $375 from $400.

"We believe that Adobe's valuation is attractive though faces greater competitive threats in the creative market and thus remains at the bottom of our pecking order in the front office market," said BMO analysts, led by Keith Bachman, in a Friday investor report.

Regarding the front office application sector, BMO recommends Salesforce (CRM) and HubSpot (HUBS) over Adobe, which both have Outperform ratings.

BMO's recent Creative Cloud Survey found that competitive dynamics are increasing, particularly among smaller businesses, students and freelancers. More than 50% of students surveyed are using rival Canva over Adobe. It also indicated that nearly 50% of freelancers are using Canva compared to about 10% using Adobe. 

Adobe's Creative Cloud is a subscription service that offers a suite of applications for design, photography, video and web creation.

"The most common answer to our tool usage question was that respondents are using both Creative Cloud and Canva, at over 50% of total respondents," BMO reported. "About 30% of users use only Canva, and about 20% of users use only Creative Cloud. Hence, there are more total users of Canva vs. Adobe. Given Adobe's preexisting market share, we view this negatively."

Recent reports indicate Canva will likely pursue an initial public offering sometime in 2026 or 2027. Canva's chief financial officer, Kelly Steckelberg, helped Zoom (ZM) go public in 2019.

"At Canva, we aspire to be a generational company, so the timing will be about when it best supports that mission," Steckelberg said in an interview last year with CFO Brew. "In the meantime, we are focused on running the business with public company standards and rhythms, because that foundation will make us stronger."

Adobe shares were down 2% during morning market action on Friday. Its share value has declined by about 20% over the past year. In comparison, the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) has ticked up 7%

data.title: Consumer sentiment improves more than expected in January
data.description:
January consumer sentiment index: 54.0 vs. 53.5 consensus and 52.9 in December, according to the initial University of Michigan Survey of Consumers data released on Friday.

Current conditions index: 52.4 vs. 50.5 forecast and 50.4 in December.

Consumer expectations index: 55.0 vs. 54.2 forecast and 54.6 prior.

Year-ahead inflation expectations: +4.2% vs. +4.2% prior.

Five-year implied inflation: +3.4% vs. +3.2% prior.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Amazon Pharmacy to offer Wegovy pill for as little as $25 a month
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) is now offering Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy (NVO) in a pill form on its pharmacy platform, capitalizing on the popularity – and proliferation -- of weight-loss medications.

Customers can acquire the recently-approved Wegovy pill for as little as $25 per month with eligible insurance plans, or through a cash-pay option for $149. Amazon Pharmacy (AMZN) currently offers Wegovy, Ozempic (NVO), and Zepbound (LLY) pen injectors with a prescription.  

“By offering Wegovy through both insurance and a straightforward cash-pay option, we’re giving people more choice, greater transparency, and fewer barriers to care,” said Tanvi Patel, general manager of Amazon Pharmacy.

Healthcare providers can submit a prescription directly to the online Amazon Pharmacy (AMZN) or to an Amazon Pharmacy kiosk, enabling patients to receive their medication as soon as the same day. Customers can also access Amazon One Medical assistance for treatment recommendations, care management for weight loss, and clinical evaluation.

Amazon Pharmacy also collaborates with healthcare providers and digital health companies like Weight Watchers (WW), Wheel, and 9amHealth to expand access to weight loss medications and treatments.

The news is weighing on shares of Hims & Hers Health (HIMS), WW International (WW), GoodRx Holdings (GDRX), and LifeMD (LFMD), all of which offer comparable weight-loss medications, including Wegovy in pill form.

data.title: BigBear.ai partners with Kraft Group
data.description:
• BigBear.ai (BBAI) on Friday announced a new partnership with the Kraft Group.
  • As part of the partnership, BigBear.ai has entered into an agreement with the Kraft Group’s International Forest Products LLC (IFP) to strengthen supply chain transparency.
  • This work is focused on enhancing IFP’s visibility into global supply chains and identifying where AI-enabled insights can improve operational performance and commercial decision-making.
  • In addition, BigBear.ai (BBAI) has entered into a marketing partnership as an official sponsor of the New England Patriots.
  • BigBear.ai (BBAI) shares were up ~4%.
